  "findings": [
    "Misdiagnosed system restriction or dirty coil as a low charge condition",
    "Incorrect refrigerant charge level based on manufacturer charging charts"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform a complete system diagnostic including superheat, subcooling, and static pressure measurements",
    "Clean the evaporator and condenser coils before adjusting refrigerant charge",
    "Verify TXV bulb placement and operation before adding or recovering refrigerant"
  ],
  "rationale": "The classic 'triple threat' diagnostic error (low charge vs. TXV starving vs. dirty coil) often leads to misdiagnosing a restricted TXV or dirty coil as a low charge. Adding refrigerant to a system with a restricted TXV or dirty coil will overcharge the system, leading to high head pressure, compressor flooding, or premature compressor failure. The system must be properly diagnosed using both superheat and subcooling measurements against the manufacturer's charging charts.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Charging Charts and Service Manuals",
    "AHRI Guideline V - Calculating Superheat and Subcooling"
  ]
}
